from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into fluent,
    natural‑language sentences. Facts about the same subject are merged into one
    sentence to produce a more complex, human‑like description.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase.
    _phrase_map = {
        "almaMater": "graduated from",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "has status",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "participated in mission",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "is a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title of",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon award",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "had crew members",
        "representative": "had representative",
        "alternativeName": "also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senators",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        if isinstance(obj, str) and len(obj) >= 2 and ((obj[0] == obj[-1] == '"') or (obj[0] == obj[-1] == "'")):
            return obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    # Group facts by their subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_facts = defaultdict(list)   # subject -> list of (phrase, object, raw_pred)

    for triple in triples:
        subj = triple.subject
        pred = triple.predicate
        obj = _clean(triple.object)

        phrase = _phrase_map.get(pred)
        if phrase:
            # Add unit for timeInSpace when missing
            if pred == "timeInSpace" and not any(unit in obj.lower() for unit in ["minute", "hour", "day"]):
                obj = f"{obj} minutes"
            subject_facts[subj].append((phrase, obj, pred))
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            subject_facts[subj].append((pred, obj, pred))

    # Build short descriptions for subjects to allow embedding (e.g., "Glen Ridge, New Jersey, which is part of Essex County, New Jersey")
    short_desc = {}
    for subj, facts in subject_facts.items():
        if facts:
            phrase, obj, _ = facts[0]   # use the first fact as a short description
            short_desc[subj] = f"{phrase} {obj}"

    sentences = []
    for subj, facts in subject_facts.items():
        clause_parts = []
        for phrase, obj, pred in facts:
            # If the object itself is a subject with a short description, embed it as a relative clause
            if obj in short_desc:
                rel_clause = f"{obj}, which {short_desc[obj]}"
                clause_parts.append(f"{phrase} {rel_clause}")
            else:
                clause_parts.append(f"{phrase} {obj}")

        if not clause_parts:
            continue

        if len(clause_parts) == 1:
            sentence = f"{subj} {clause_parts[0]}."
        else:
            # Join clauses: use commas between all but the last two, then "and" without Oxford comma
            if len(clause_parts) == 2:
                body = " and ".join(clause_parts)
            else:
                body = ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1]
            sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine all subject sentences into one complex sentence when possible
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0]
    else:
        # Use a semicolon to link multiple subject clauses for better flow
        return "; ".join(sentences) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
